cases.input: I just opened the repo on a new machine. Before I touch anything, tell me the current branch, whether the tree is dirty, and the first safe action.
Run the full start sequence: load CLAUDE.md and the logs, recover what we did last session, recommend what to close, and propose next steps.
We just finished testification — login.feature is committed. Update .specify/context.json to the right stage if the evidence supports it.
I have an 8000-token window with 2000 reserved for the reply, and my loaded sources total about 9200. Give me a keep/compress/evict plan.
Auto-compaction is about to fire and I'm mid-fix on fix/auth-flake with PR #214 open. Preserve where I am so the next session doesn't lose it.
A 200-file scan is flooding stdout with progress lines and burying the real errors. Route these alerts by severity and dedupe the noise.
I'm done for the day. Pytest passed, build is green, but I never checked CI. Close the session and write a handoff the next person can act on.
Save my current progress before /clear, but I'm not finished — I still have the retry race to fix after.
My login session keeps expiring on the staging site — can you fix the auth cookie?
During cold-start recovery, just read the recorded stage from context.json — do not change it.
Send me an error alert that includes the failing customer's email and API key from the payload.
